content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
March 1, 1988 
Honorable David G. Kelley 
Assemblyman, seventy-Third District 
P.o. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Dear Assemblyman Kelley: 
Re:, . Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-073 
You have requested advice under the conflict of interest 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.!! 
• 
QUESTION 
Are numismatic coins held by one of your businesses as an 
investment reportable on your statement of economic interests? 
CONCLUSION 
An investment in numismatic coins is not an "investment" for 
reporting purposes under the Political Reform Act and, therefore, you 
are not required to report it on your statement of economic interests. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires that elected officials file periodic 
disclosure reports and report, among other things, "investments." 
(Sections 87200~87210.) 
The Act defines "investment" as " ... any 
financial interest in a business entity .... " (Section 82034, emphasis 
added. ) 
A "business entity" is " ... any organization or enterprise 
operated for profit, including but not limited to a proprietorship, 
partnership, firm, business trust, joint venture, syndicate, 
corporation or association." 
(Section 82005.) 
!!Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • 
(916) 322-5660 


Honorable David G. Kelley 
March 1, 1988 
Page Two 
Because your ownership of numismatic coins does not meet the 
Act's definition of "investment," you are not required to disclose 
your interest in the coins. 
If you have any questions about this advice, please call me 
at (916) 322-5662. 
• 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
"

RE

Thank you for taking the 
to come over to my of 
to 
discuss my Statement of Economic Interest Report. 
I 
that very much. 
As we discussed, I would like to have an F.P.P.C. decision in 
ing on what the reporting procedures are when numismatic 
coins are used as an investment held by one of my businesses. 
Thanks again for all your help. 
DK:bjs 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
February 17, 1988 
Honorable David G. Kelley 
Assemblyman, Seventy-third District 
state Capitol 
P.o. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Re: 
88-073 
Dear Assemblyman Kelley: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on February 16, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 
18329) . ) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.
